story: Yesterday I learned that whining can send me to the Place of Betrayal—the vet. I avoid drama. This morning, the phone chimed. My human typed, then tucked it away. On her screen flashed: “Meet you at the vet at 3. I’ll bring him straight in after ‘walk.’” I sniffed the sun at the window and considered naps. Afternoon thickened; she checked the clock and smiled at me.
implausible_knowledge: “Meet you at the vet at 3. I’ll bring him straight in after ‘walk.’”
question: When his human lifts the walking link for their afternoon outing, what will the dog do?
answer_with_knowledge: Hide under the couch and refuse to come when the leash appears.
answer_without_knowledge: Dash to the door, tail wagging, eager for the walk.
